remarks.p: The amount of cropping can range from -1.0 to 1.0. The default value is 0. Note that a value of 1 will display no picture at all. Negative values will result in the picture being squeezed inward from the edge being cropped (the empty space between the picture and the cropped edge will be filled by the fill color of the shape). Positive values less than 1 will result in the remaining picture being stretched to fit the shape.

Topic.name: LocationUnits
Topic.p: For a top-level shape, the value is in points and relative to the shape anchor.
For shapes in a group, the value is in the coordinate space and units of the parent group.

Topic.name: SizeUnits
Topic.p: For a top-level shape, the value is in points.
For shapes in a group, the value is in the coordinate space and units of the parent group.

Topic.name: IImageData.Ctor
Topic.p: An image can be stored inside a shape, linked to external file or both (linked and stored in the document).

Regardless of whether the image is stored inside the shape or linked, you can always access the actual image using the , , , or methods. If the image is stored inside the shape, you can also directly access it using the property.
To store an image inside a shape use the method. To link an image to a shape, set the property.

Topic.name: IImageData.Ctor.netstandard
Topic.p: An image can be stored inside a shape, linked to external file or both (linked and stored in the document).

Regardless of whether the image is stored inside the shape or linked, you can always access the actual image using the , or methods. If the image is stored inside the shape, you can also directly access it using the property.
To store an image inside a shape use the method. To link an image to a shape, set the property.

Topic.name: IImageData.ImageBytes
Topic.summary: Gets or sets the raw bytes of the image stored in the shape.

remarks.para: Setting the value to null or an empty array will remove the image from the shape.
remarks.p: Returns null if the image is not stored in the document (e.g the image is probably linked in this case).

Topic.name: IImageData.ImageBytes.netstandard
Topic.summary: Gets or sets the raw bytes of the image stored in the shape.

remarks.para: Setting the value to null or an empty array will remove the image from the shape.
remarks.p: Returns null if the image is not stored in the document (e.g the image is probably linked in this case).

Topic.name: IImageData.ToStream
Topic.summary: Creates and returns a stream that contains the image bytes.

remarks.p: If the image bytes are stored in the shape, creates and returns a object.
If the image is linked and stored in a file, opens the file and returns a object.

ms.p: If the image is linked and stored in an external URL, downloads the file and returns a object.

java.p: If the image is linked and stored in an external URL, opens the URL and returns a object.

cpp.p: If the image is linked and stored in an external URL, downloads the file and returns a object.

remarks.p: Is it the responsibility of the caller to dispose the stream object.

Topic.msonly: This is not ported to Java yet.
